accurate.why: The bill does not say the agency must provide certification 'within 10 days to the Office of Advocacy' — the amendment says the agency must act 'not later than 10 days after completing the certification' (implying publication/notice) but the bill text amends section 605(b) without specifying it goes to the Office of Advocacy specifically.

no_hallucinations.why: The description of SBA Office of Advocacy's role is accurate real-world context; no fabricated claims.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill as expanding RFA requirements for small businesses.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Accurately describes the expansion of initial regulatory flexibility analysis requirements and petition process.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes the specified petition content requirements and agency non-participation penalty.

effective_dates.why: No specific effective date stated; omission appropriate.

major_provisions_covered.why: The CRS summary omits the Section 609 guidance publication requirement and the Section 610 periodic review reforms, which are significant provisions.

correct_entities.why: SBA Office of Advocacy and Chief Counsel correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: No specific figures stated in the CRS summary.

judged_via: claude-sonnet-4-6 via parallel Claude Code subagents (web/knowledge-verified context)
